p1|CUL.summary: Perception of national economic situation compared to one year ago (better or worse).

p2|CUL.summary: Expectation of national economic situation in the next year.

p3|CUL.summary: Word choice to describe the current political situation of the country.

p7|CUL.summary: Assessment of whether the country needs no changes, small changes, profound changes, or radical change.

p8|CUL.summary: Frequency of consuming news or programs about politics or public affairs.

p9_1|CUL.summary: Primary source of information about national events (first mention).

p11|CUL.summary: Perception of how much politics relates to daily life.

p12|CUL.summary: Importance of politics to the respondent.

p13_1|CUL.summary: Attribution of responsibility for judging criminals.

p14_1|CUL.summary: Left-right ideological placement of the Catholic Church (1-5 scale).

p15_1|CUL.summary: Left-right ideological placement of the PAN party (1-5 scale).

p16|CUL.summary: Respondent's own left-right ideological self-placement (1-5 scale).

p17|CUL.summary: Choice between freedom and security as core values.

p18|CUL.summary: Source of anxiety or fear regarding public security in the respondent's state.

p19_1|CUL.summary: Preference between paired statements on political values (first pair).

p21|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether politics contributes to improving the standard of living for all Mexicans.

p65|CUL.summary: Agreement with the statement that breaking the law is sometimes necessary to act correctly.

p23|CUL.summary: Choice between implementing a good policy that creates conflict or avoiding conflict by not implementing it.

p80_2|CUL.summary: Agreement with the statement that people in the community care only about their own well-being.

p25|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether political participation is worthwhile.

p26|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether Chamber of Deputies discussions are important to citizens or only to politicians.

p27|CUL.summary: Agreement with selecting part of the Chamber of Deputies through lottery of non-partisan citizens.

p28|CUL.summary: Identification of which person best represents the respondent's interests.

p29|CUL.summary: Opinion on the purpose or function of democracy.

p30|CUL.summary: Most important aspect for a society to be considered a democracy.

p31|CUL.summary: Preference for how the country should be governed.

p32|CUL.summary: Assessment of how democratic Mexico is today.

p33|CUL.summary: Importance of Mexico being governed democratically (0-10 scale).

p34|CUL.summary: Rating of how democratically Mexico is currently being governed (0-10 scale).

p36|CUL.summary: Agreement with statements about democracy and governance.

p38|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether government should follow Mexican Revolution ideas or change them.

p39|CUL.summary: Preference for creating a new constitution, partial changes, or keeping it as is.

p40|CUL.summary: Belief in the extent to which the constitution is upheld in Mexico.

p41|CUL.summary: Assessment of the quality of elections in Mexico.

p42|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether INE guarantees clean electoral processes.

p43|CUL.summary: Belief in whether state elections will be more or less reliable now that INE oversees them.

p44_1|CUL.summary: Perception of the extent to which one can start any business.

p45_1|CUL.summary: Most important quality children should have (first pair in paired-choice exercise).

p46|CUL.summary: Belief in citizens' ability to influence government decisions.

p48_1|CUL.summary: Participation in meeting with authorities in the past year.

p49|CUL.summary: Opinion on a specific political statement or viewpoint.

p50|CUL.summary: Opinion on what is more important in a political context.

p51|CUL.summary: Satisfaction with current opportunities for political participation or desire for more forms.

p51a|CUL.summary: Follow-up specifying desired additional forms of political participation.

p52|CUL.summary: Satisfaction with how much one's opinions are taken into account.

p54_1|CUL.summary: Perception of what neighbors typically do when authorities fail to solve problems (first action).

p55_1|CUL.summary: Agreement with neighbors organizing blockades and marches as a response to unresolved problems.

p55_2|CUL.summary: Agreement with neighbors turning to media as a response to unresolved problems.

p55_3|CUL.summary: Agreement with neighbors approaching other authorities as a response to unresolved problems.

p55_4|CUL.summary: Agreement with neighbors approaching a political party as a response to unresolved problems.

p55_5|CUL.summary: Agreement with neighbors organizing among themselves as a response to unresolved problems.

p55_7|CUL.summary: Agreement with other unspecified neighbor responses to unresolved problems.

p55|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether committing damages or harm is justified to defend a just cause.

p56_1|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether authorities should make exceptions in law enforcement for protests by homemakers.

p57_1|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether government should intervene in decisions about school curriculum.

p58_1|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether government should make public or keep secret information about government spending.

p59|CUL.summary: Preference for solving community problems through community action or government intervention.

p60|CUL.summary: Opinion on the legitimacy of decisions made by majority vote.

p61|CUL.summary: Truth or falsity of the statement that a strong leader can do more for the country than laws.

p63_1|CUL.summary: Perception of how much government administers justice.

p64|CUL.summary: Opinion on how authorities should act in pursuit of justice.

p66_1|CUL.summary: Most important factor dividing Mexicans today.

p66_2|CUL.summary: Second most important factor dividing Mexicans today.

p68_1|CUL.summary: Voting participation in the 2012 presidential election.

p69_1|CUL.summary: Party voted for in the 2012 presidential election.

p69_3|CUL.summary: Party voted for in the 2006 presidential election.

p71|CUL.summary: Opinion on whether political parties buy votes.

p72|CUL.summary: Experience of being offered something in exchange for one's vote.

p73|CUL.summary: Importance of candidate versus party when deciding how to vote.

p74_1|CUL.summary: Agreement with the statement that political parties criticize each other but are all essentially the same.

p75|CUL.summary: Agreement with statements about political parties.

p76|CUL.summary: Main cause of conflicts after elections.

p77|CUL.summary: Best way for a citizen to contribute to solving Mexico's political and social problems.

p79|CUL.summary: Strength of identification with a political party (very or somewhat).
